criteria.label: Define regression and classification and state what differs (target y, loss, metrics).
criteria.example: Write: regression predicts continuous y with MSE/MAE; classification predicts discrete classes with cross-entropy and precision/recall/F1.

criteria.label: Classify three problem descriptions as regression, classification, or neither.
criteria.example: Given house price, spam detection, and customer clustering tasks, label each as regression, classification, or not supervised classification/regression.

criteria.label: Explain why logistic regression is classification despite its name.
criteria.example: One sentence: it outputs P(class|x) trained with cross-entropy; "regression" refers to log-odds, not continuous y.

criteria.label: Match output head and loss to problem type in code or pseudocode.
criteria.example: For K-way image labels, specify softmax logits + CrossEntropyLoss; for price prediction, linear output + MSELoss.

criteria.label: Identify a mis-framed problem (e.g. MSE on class IDs) and propose the correct framing.
criteria.example: Spot digit 0–9 trained with LinearRegression; recommend multi-class softmax classifier and cross-entropy instead.
